profile.profileDescription: Installation profile for Imagining Futures repo

slogans

item.value: Viet Nam Chien Thang Dai Dich

label.name_singular: Viet Nam Chien Thang Dai Dich
label.name_plural: Viet Nam Chien Thang Dai Dich
label.description: Viet Nam Chien Thang Dai Dich-Vietnam wins the pandemic

Attribution---This license lets others distribute, remix, adapt, and build upon your work, even commercially, as long as they credit you for the original creation. This is the most accommodating of licenses offered. Recommended for maximum dissemination and use of licensed materials.

Attribution-ShareAlike---This license lets others remix, adapt, and build upon your work even for commercial purposes, as long as they credit you and license their new creations under the identical terms. This license is often compared to “copyleft” free and open source software licenses. All new works based on yours will carry the same license, so any derivatives will also allow commercial use. This is the license used by Wikipedia, and is recommended for materials that would benefit from incorporating content from Wikipedia and similarly licensed projects.
Attribution-NoDerivs---This license lets others reuse the work for any purpose, including commercially; however, it cannot be shared with others in adapted form, and credit must be provided to you.

Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike---This license lets others remix, adapt, and build upon your work non-commercially, as long as they credit you and license their new creations under the identical terms.

Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivs---This license is the most restrictive of our six main licenses, only allowing others to download your works and share them with others as long as they credit you, but they can’t change them in any way or use them commercially.

Public Domain--- is a public dedication tool, which allows creators to give up their copyright and put their works into the worldwide public domain. CC0 allows reusers to distribute, remix, adapt, and build upon the material in any medium or format, with no conditions.

Highly sensitive – refers to resource that cannot be viewed, downloaded, print nor accessed. The resources in this category are confidential. The depositor, persons connected to the project and repository administrators may have access to the resource.

Moderately sensitive – refers to resources that are to be viewed only, not to be downloaded and print. Retrievers interested in accessing the resource need to fill ‘Data Retrieval Form’ to justify the need for the resource. Data Retrieval Form to be assessed and approved by the Principal Investigator and co-PIs
Low sensitive – refers to resources that are to be shared openly. Retrievers will be able to view, download, print and use
https://pleiades.stoa.org/
https://www.getty.edu/research/tools/vocabularies/tgn/index.html
https://imaginingfutures.world/
https://orcid.org/
Virtual International Authority File
indicates that the Item labeled with this Rights Statement is in copyright.
indicates that the Item labeled with this Rights Statement has been identified as an ‘Orphan Work’ under the terms of the EU Orphan Works Directive.

label.name_singular: In Copyright - Rights-holder(s) Unlocatable or Unidentifiable (InC-RUU)
label.name_plural: In Copyright - Rights-holder(s) Unlocatable or Unidentifiable (InC-RUU)
label.description: indicates that the Item labeled with this Rights Statement has been identified as in copyright, but whose rights-holder(s) either cannot be identified or cannot be located.
indicates that the Item labeled with this Rights Statement is in copyright but that educational use is allowed without the need to obtain additional permission.
indicates that the Item labeled with this Rights Statement is in copyright but that non-commercial use is allowed without the need to obtain additional permission.
indicates that the underlying Work is in the Public Domain, but the organization that has published the Item is contractually required to allow only non-commercial use by third parties.
indicates that the underlying Work is in the Public Domain, but the organization that has published the Item is contractually required to restrict certain forms of use by third parties.

label.name_singular: No Copyright - Other Known Legal Restrictions (NoC-OKLR)
label.name_plural: No Copyright - Other Known Legal Restrictions (NoC-OKLR)
label.description: indicates that the underlying Work is in the Public Domain, but that laws other than copyright impose restrictions on the use of the Item by third parties.

indicates that the underlying Work is in the Public Domain under the laws of the United States, but that a determination was not made as to its copyright status under the copyright laws of other countries.
indicates that the organization that has published the Item believes that no copyright or related rights are known to exist, but that a conclusive determination could not be made.
indicates that the organization that has published the Item has not evaluated the copyright and related rights status of the Item.
indicates that the organization that has made the Item available has reviewed the copyright and related rights status of the Item, but was unable to determine the copyright status of the Item.

label.name: Introduction
label.description: Narrative description for set. This is the primary text used when publicly presenting the set.

label.name: Item description
label.description: Narrative description for item in this set. This is the primary text used when publicly presenting the item in the context of this set.

label.name: Presentation type
label.description: Determines how set is rendered when displayed to the public in the Pawtucket/Pawtucket2 front-end.

label.name: Is primary item for set?
label.description: The item marked as primary will be used to represent the set as a whole when included in a list of sets.

label.name: Chronology element code
label.description: Element code of date range element to be used when placing set items into chronological order.

label.name: Authorization date
label.description: The date when the loan was authorized.

label.name: Loan in date
label.description: The date a loan will enter the institution.

label.name: Loan out date
label.description: The date a loan will enter the institution.

label.name: Loan return date
label.description: The date a loan is due back to the lending institution.

label.name: Loan renewal application date
label.description: The date to apply for a renewal to the loan.

label.name: Loan conditions
label.description: A statement of the conditions on a loan.

label.name: Loan note
label.description: General information about the loan.

label.name: Loan purpose
label.description: General information about the purpose of loan.

label.name: Movement method
label.description: The method used in the movement of an object.

label.name: Movement note
label.description: Additional information about the movement of an object or group of objects.

label.name: Planned removal date
label.description: The date an object is to be removed from its current location.

label.name: Planned removal date
label.description: The date an object was actually removed from its current location.

label.name: Reason for move
label.description: The purpose or reason for the movement of the object or group of objects.

label.name: Introduction
label.description: Narrative description for tour. This is the primary text used when publicly presenting the tour.

label.name: Introduction
label.description: Narrative description for tour stop. This is the primary text used when publicly presenting the stop.

label.name: Georeference
label.description: Location of this stop.

label.name: Description
label.description: An account of the resource.

label.name: Source of description
label.description: Source of item description. This should be a formal citation if possible. For informal sources a narrative description is acceptable.

label.name: Caption
label.description: Short descriptive caption for item.

label.name: Georeference
label.description: Coordinates documenting the location of the item

label.name: Notes (internal use only)
label.description: Notes regarding item

label.name: Library of Congress Subject Headings
label.description: Library of Congress Subject headings describing this object. To add a new heading simply type the first few letters of the heading and choose the desired one from the displayed list of possible matches.

label.name: Repository
label.description: This element identifies the name and location of the repository that hold the materials being described.

label.name: Dates
label.description: Record at least one of the following types of dates for the unit of description, as appropriate to the materials and the level of description

label.name: Extent and medium of the unit
label.description: Record the extent of the unit of description by giving the number of physical or logical units in arabic numerals and the unit of measurement. Give the specific medium (media) of the unit of description.

label.name: Administrative/Biographical History Element
label.description: Record concisely any significant information on the origin, progress, development and work of the organization (or organizations) or on the life and work of the individual (or individuals) responsible for the creation of the unit of description. If additional information is available in a published source, cite the source.

label.name: Scope and Content
label.description: Give a summary of the scope (such as, time periods, geography) and content, (such as documentary forms, subject matter, administrative processes) of the unit of description, appropriate to the level of description.

label.name: System of Arrangement
label.description: Specify the internal structure, order and/or the system of classification of the unit of description. Note how these have been treated by the archivist. For electronic records, record or reference information on system design.

label.name: Conditions Governing Access
label.description: Specify the law or legal status, contract, regulation or policy that affects access to the unit of description. Indicate the extent of the period of closure and the date at which the material will open when appropriate.

label.name: Physical Access
label.description: Indicate any important physical conditions, such as preservation requirements, that affect the use of the unit of description. Note any software and/or hardware required to access the unit of description.

label.name: Technical Access
label.description: Indicate any important physical conditions, such as preservation requirements, that affect the use of the unit of description. Note any software and/or hardware required to access the unit of description.

label.name: Conditions Governing Reproduction
label.description: Give information about conditions, such as copyright, governing the reproduction of the unit of description after access has been provided. If the existence of such conditions is unknown, record this. If there are no conditions, no statement is necessary.

label.name: Languages and Scripts of Material
label.description: Record the language(s) and/or script(s) of the materials comprising the unit of description. Note any distinctive alphabets, scripts, symbol systems or abbreviations employed.

label.name: Finding Aids
label.description: Give information about any finding aids that the repository or records creator may have that provide information relating to the context and contents of the unit of description. If appropriate, include information on where to obtain a copy.

label.name: Archival History
label.description: Record the successive transfers of ownership, responsibility and/or custody of the unit of description and indicate those actions, such as history of the arrangement, production of contemporary finding aids, re-use of the records for other purposes or software migrations, that have contributed to its present structure and arrangement. Give the dates of these actions, insofar as they can be ascertained. If the archival history is unknown, record that information.

label.name: Immediate Source of Acquisition or Transfer
label.description: Record the source from which the unit of description was acquired and the date and/or method of acquisition if any or all of this information is not confidential. If the source is unknown, record that information. Optionally, add accession numbers or codes.

label.name: Appraisal, Destruction and Scheduling
label.description: Record appraisal, destruction and scheduling actions taken on or planned for the unit of description, especially if they may affect the interpretation of the material.

label.name: Accruals
label.description: Indicate if accruals are expected. Where appropriate, give an estimate of their quantity and frequency.

label.name: Existence and Location of Originals
label.description: If the original of the unit of description is available (either in the institution or elsewhere) record its location, together with any significant control numbers. If the originals no longer exist, or their location is unknown, give that information.

label.name: Existence and Location of Copies
label.description: If the copy of the unit of description is available (either in the institution or elsewhere) record its location, together with any significant control numbers.

label.name: Related Units of Description
label.description: Record information about units of description in the same repository or elsewhere that are related by provenance or other association(s). Use appropriate introductory wording and explain the nature of the relationship . If the related unit of description is a finding aid, use the finding aids element of description (3.4.5) to make the reference to it.

label.name: Publication Note
label.description: Record a citation to, and/or information about a publication that is about or based on the use, study, or analysis of the unit of description. Include references to published facsimiles or transcriptions.

label.name: Notes
label.description: Record specialized or other important information not accommodated by any of the defined elements of description.

label.name: Archivist Note
label.description: Record notes on sources consulted in preparing the description and who prepared it.

label.name: Rules or Conventions
label.description: Record the international, national and/or local rules or conventions followed in preparing the description.

label.name: Date(s) of descriptions
label.description: Record the date(s) the entry was prepared and/or revised.

label.name: Administrative/Biographical History
label.description: Record information relevant to the understanding of the life, activities, and relationships of the person or family.

label.name: Places of residence
label.description: Indicate the geographical place(s) of residence of the person or family and the length of residence in each place, as well as any other place with which the person or family has a connection.

label.name: Education
label.description: Record information about the formal education of persons, including members of families whose education is important to an understanding of their life.

label.name: Occupation
label.description: Record information about the principal occupation(s) and career or lifework of persons or about the activities of families. Also indicate any other activities important to an understanding of the life of the person or family.

label.name: Other Significant Information
label.description: Record any other important information not recorded elsewhere in the biographical history.

label.name: Administrative History
label.description: Give information relevant to the understanding of the creator's functions, activities, and relations with other corporate bodies.

label.name: Dates of Founding and/or Dissolution
label.description: Give the date and place of the founding of the corporate body, and if applicable the date and place of its dissolution.

label.name: Geographical Areas
label.description: Give the location of the head office and of any branch or regional offices as well as the geographic region in which the organization operated.

label.name: Mandate
label.description: Record the enabling legislation or other legal policy instrument(s) that act as the source of authority for the corporate body in terms of its powers, responsibilities, or sphere of activities, including any significant changes in its authority and functions.

label.name: Functions
label.description: Record information about the functions and activities performed by the corporate body being described.

label.name: Administrative Structure
label.description: Describe the internal structure of the corporate body and the dates of any changes to the structure that are significant to the understanding of the way the corporate body conducts its affairs.

label.name: Predecessor and Successor Bodies
label.description: Give the facts of the relationship of the body with predecessor or successor bodies to its mandate, functions, or activities.

label.name: Names of Chief Officers
label.description: Record the personal name(s) of persons holding the position as chief officer

label.name: Predecessor and Successor Bodies
label.description: Give the facts of the relationship of the body with predecessor or successor bodies to its mandate, functions, or activities.

label.name: Format and extent of the record
label.description: Digital format of the file. Choose between Video, Audio, Document, 3D, Image.

label.name: Digital Format
label.description: Digital format of the file. Choose between Video, Audio, Document, 3D, Image.

label.name: Digital Format
label.description: Digital format of the file. Choose between Video, Audio, Document, 3D, Image.

label.name: Slogans
label.description: Slogans

label.name: Cultural Group
label.description: The cultural or ethnic group connected to the resource. Provide the names of all the cultural or ethnic group/community connected to the resource. This could be more than one. E.g. Arabs, Assyrians, Balanta

label.name: Cultural Context
label.description: The cultural event or context associated with the resource. Describe the cultural context or event associated with the resource. This descriptor responds to ‘What was happening when the resource was produced?’, ‘What event or practice is the resource recording?’ A list of suggested events is provided but add additional, more specific values to the vocabulary. E.g. Dancing, migration, singing.

label.name: Social Group
label.description: The social group associated with or recorded by the resource. Describe the social group associated with the resource. This descriptor responds to ‘What is the description of the people recorded by the resources?’ A list of suggested social group is provided but add additional, more specific values to the vocabulary. e.g. https://simplicable.com/society/social-groups. e.g. Classmates, Family, Committees.

label.name: Cultural Sensitivity
label.description: This refers to the attributed standard that governs the content and knowledge of the resources and who can access the resource. Select the level of cultural sensitivity of the content and knowledge of the resource. Consider this carefully before making your selection. ---Highly sensitive – refers to resource that cannot be viewed, downloaded, print nor accessed. The resources in this category are confidential. The depositor, persons connected to the project and repository administrators may have access to the resource. ;Moderately sensitive – refers to resources that are to be viewed only, not to be downloaded and print. Retrievers interested in accessing the resource need to fill ‘Data Retrieval Form’ to justify the need for the resource. Data Retrieval Form to be assessed and approved by the Principal Investigator and co-PIs ;Low sensitivity – refers to resources that are to be shared openly. Retrievers will be able to view, download, print and use

label.name: Access Restriction
label.description: This refers to availability of the resource. Select the level of access that is appropriate to the resource. Consider this carefully before making your selection. ---Yes – refers to resources that are restricted. If you choose ‘Yes’, you need to justify ;No – refers to resources with no access restriction. Resources will be made openly available.

label.name: Reasons for Restriction
label.description: Justification for limiting access to the resources. The field is mandatory if the value for ‘Access restriction’ is Yes. Provide justifications for limiting the access to the resource and specify the consequences and potential impacts of making the resource accessible. Specify if access to the resource should ne restricted permanently or for a specific period of time. The image records various civil protests and disobedience is countries that are repressive. Making the image accessible could make the persons connected to the image targets. Therefore, access to the image should be permanently restricted.

label.name: Use and Reproduction
label.description: Shows users how to cite this data when they want to reproduce or refer to it. A spesific, permenent and searchable info about this data (such as ID) or a perminent url can be used. E.g. UFC0031_1.21

label.name: Alternative Title
label.description: The name given to the resource by the Creator (depositor) in other language. Provide a short title for the resource in other language. Do not use any punctuation. Only capitalize the first alphabet of the project title, acronyms and any personal and geographic names. Aso wiwo awon obinrin ile Yoruba.

label.name: License
label.description: Information about the shareability, distribution and adaptation of the resource. Select which licence is applicable to the resource. Consider this carefully before making your selection. CC BY-NC 4.0 - This license requires that reusers give credit to the creator. It allows reusers to distribute, remix, adapt, and build upon the material in any medium or format, for noncommercial purposes only.;CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 - This license requires that reusers give credit to the creator. It allows reusers to copy and distribute the material in any medium or format in unadapted form and for noncommercial purposes only. Non-Open Access – This resource is anot allowed to be shared, remix, adapt or redistribute.

label.name: Term in other language
label.description: Translation of the language of the term

label.name: Authority File
label.description: One or multiple links to different authority sources online, such as wikidata, viaf, getty, FAST.

label.name: Languages
label.description: Languages spoken by the person. Native or other.

label.name: Project Webpage (IF)
label.description: Project's web page URL on IF Website.

label.name: Project Website (Other)
label.description: Project's website other than its page on IF Website.

label.name: Region
label.description: IF region terms.

label.name: Keywords
label.description: Descriptive words that are most significant and unique to the resource.

label.name: Right ownership
label.description: Information about the individual/community/groups/organisation who owns the rights in and over the resource. Select the participant or community that owns the rights in and over the resource. You can add names of individual/community/organisation that are not on the list.

label.name: Production Technique
label.description: Describe the technique used in producing the resource. This descriptor responds to ‘How was the resource produced?’ A list of relevant production technique is provided but additional techniques can be added. E.g. Photography, report writing, audio recording, video recording.

label.name: Equipment used
label.description: The equipment used directly for the production of the resource. List all the equipment used for the production of the resource. Provide the general names of each equipment, separated with commas. Tripod stand, DSLR camera, Video recording

label.name: AI
label.description: Machine Learning Model or Artificial Intelligence Model used to translate, enhance, transform, or create the resource.

label.name: ai
label.description: Machine Learning Model or Artificial Intelligence Model used to translate, enhance, transform, or create the resource.

label.name: Original ID
label.description: International or local identifiers associated with the original material. International or local identifiers associated with the original material.

role.name: Administrators
role.description: Confers actions needed by those administering the system

role.name: Cataloguers
role.description: Confers actions needed by those editing catalogue entries

role.name: Researcher
role.description: Allows users to only search and view - but not edit - catalogue entries

role.name: User content moderator
role.description: Allows users to moderate user generated tags and comments

group.name: Cataloguers
group.description: Users who can add and edit catalogue entries
